threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the London Overground' instead of just 'the Overground.' Everyone else here knows it’s just part of life, not a tourist attraction.
Caught a glimpse of a bloke serenading his mates with a tinny on the 35 last night. Only in Brixton would that feel like a normal Tuesday.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Spotted an old lady in a bright orange tracksuit walking her ferret on a leash by the Arminiusmarkthalle. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can grab a late-night döner while watching a spontaneous techno party erupt on the street. Only in Berlin do late-night snacks come with a side of bass.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their nonchalance when the Muni is late, while the newbies are pacing like it's the end of the world.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy at the Powell station juggling while waiting for the N-Judah. Just another day on Muni, merging art with transit chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell but miss out on the real vibrancy of Gràcia's local plazas, where the true essence of the neighborhood thrives.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll often see them staring at their phones in the middle of the street, trying to navigate while missing the beauty around them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look baffled when a double-decker bus zooms past your head at the stop. Locals just know to step back.
Last night, the 37 bus was packed with a silent dance party. Two guys in the back were belting out 90s hits like it was the last train home.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard someone casually discussing the best kebab shops while waiting for the tram at Karl-Marx-Straße, I felt at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can spot an impromptu rave in a park, complete with sound systems, while kids are just a few meters away on a playground. Only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name the exact spot of the last Mission taco truck closing, and it’s not the one you’d expect.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus stopping mid-block yesterday to let a guy chase after his hat. The driver just smiled and waited. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think this area is all about Gaudí, but the real charm lies in the little plazas where locals gather for vermut, not the big crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a recent arrival by their utter confusion over the metro's color-coded map; locals know the shortcuts through the labyrinth.
